12.reason: [The loop condition should check for EOF or the termination value -1 as per problem description. However, the problem states that -1 represents end of operations, so the loop should break when op == -1, not rely solely on scanf result. Also, scanf returns number of items read, so comparing to -1 is incorrect; it should be compared to EOF or checked if op == -1 inside the loop.]

Also, scanf returns number of items read, so comparing to -1 is incorrect; it should be compared to EOF or checked if op == -1 inside the loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for EOF, not -1. scanf returns EOF on end-of-file or error, not -1 for the operation -1. This causes the loop to continue even after reading -1, leading to incorrect behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for EOF or the termination value -1 as per problem description; however, the problem states that -1 ends operations, so reading should stop when op == -1, not continue. Also, scanf returns number of items read, not the value -1 on end-of-input. This causes infinite loop or incorrect behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for EOF or the sentinel value -1 correctly. According to the problem, -1 indicates end of operations, but this loop continues even after reading -1 because it only stops when scanf returns -1 (which is EOF on some systems). However, the bigger issue is that when op == -1, the code still calls pop(stack), which is incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for EOF, not -1. scanf returns EOF on end-of-file or error, not -1 for the input -1. This causes the loop to continue even after reading -1, leading to incorrect behavior.]

20.reason: [This else block executes for both op == 0 and op == -1. When op == -1 (end of operations), it incorrectly calls pop(), which will print "error " if stack is empty or pop an element, violating the requirement that -1 only terminates input.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This executes for op == 0 AND op == -1. When op is -1 (end marker), it incorrectly calls pop(), which may print "error " or a value. The program should break on op == -1, not treat it as pop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This executes for op == 0 AND op == -1. When op is -1 (end marker), it incorrectly performs a pop operation instead of terminating.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This executes pop for any op != 1, including -1 (end marker) and invalid ops. It should only pop when op == 0.]
